titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD 4.3.Sacr.2 (previously, Misc. Ch. 716)

p.seg: To all the sons of Holy Mother Church John de Vaux sends greeting.

Let present and future know that I have granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to Saint Mary and to Saint Cuthbert, and to the monks of Durham, that land in the territory of Gullane, which William de Vaux my father gave to them in pure and perpetual alms. And I will, that Luke the son of Osmund and his own heirs should hold that land of the same monks by the same boundaries by which the same Osmund held that land on that day in which he was alive and dead, and with all its easements and just pertinents, freely, quietly, fully, and honourably, just as the charter of the said William my father, made thereupon for the said monks, and Osmund and his heirs, bears witness, rendering thereupon annually to the said monks seven shillings at the feast of Saint Michael.
With these witnesses : Robert de Vaux of Elbottle, William of Munfichet, Walter of Lindsay, Walter of Fenton, Reginald of Hoton.
